Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2024-08-05 section=business period_end=2024-06-30
Item 1. Business.
The Procter & Gamble Company (the Company) is a world-leading multinational consumer goods company focused on providing trusted, branded products of superior quality, performance and value to improve the lives of consumers around the world - now and for generations to come. Our products are sold in about 180 countries and territories throughout the world. The Company was incorporated in Ohio in 1905, having first been established as a New Jersey corporation in 1890, and was built from a business founded in Cincinnati in 1837 by William Procter and James Gamble.
Additional information required by this item is incorporated herein by reference to Management's Discussion and Analysis (MD&A); and Notes 1 and 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements. Unless the context indicates otherwise, the terms "Company," "P&G," "we," "our" or "us" as used herein refer to The Procter & Gamble Company (the registrant) and its subsidiaries. Throughout this Form 10-K, we incorporate by reference information from other documents filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
The Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K, quarterly reports on Form 10-Q and current reports on Form 8-K, and amendments thereto, are filed electronically with the SEC. The SEC maintains an internet site that contains these reports at: www.sec.gov. Reports can also be accessed and downloaded through links from our website at: www.pginvestor.com. P&G includes the website link solely as a textual reference and the information on our website is not incorporated by reference into this report. Copies of these reports are also available, without charge, by contacting EQ Shareowner Services, 1100 Centre Pointe Curve, Suite 101, Mendota, MN 55120-4100.
Financial Information about Segments
Information about our reportable segments can be found in the MD&A and Note 2 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Narrative Description of Business
Business Model
. Our business model is focused on delivering sustainable value creation by driving balanced top- and bottom-line growth. We create, manufacture, market and distribute a diversified portfolio of daily-use products to delight consumers with irresistible superiority across five key vectors - product performance, packaging, brand communication, retail execution and value. We invest in research and development and consumer insights to invent new categories or products and innovate our existing products, ensuring they meet evolving consumer needs and preferences. We leverage marketing strategies including advertising, promotions and endorsements to drive brand awareness and loyalty among consumers. The Company utilizes various distribution channels, including retail stores, e-commerce platfor
…[truncated 2964 chars]…
ignificant market share position. Our integrated strategy and our focus on driving superiority across product, packaging, brand communication, retail execution and value are key differentiators in the marketplace.
2        The Procter & Gamble Company
Government Regulation
. Our Company is subject to a wide variety of laws and regulations across the countries in which we do business. In the United States, many of our products and manufacturing operations are subject to one or more federal or state regulatory agencies, including the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA), the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC). We are also subject to anti-corruption laws and regulations, such as the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and antitrust and competition laws and regulations that govern our dealings with suppliers, customers, competitors and government officials.
In addition, many foreign jurisdictions in which we do business have regulations and regulatory bodies that govern similar aspects of our operations and products, in some cases to an even more significant degree. We are also subject to expanding laws and regulations related to environmental protection and other sustainability-related matters, non-financial reporting and diligence, labor and employment, trade, taxation and privacy and data protection, including the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation and similar regulations in states within the United States and in countries around the world.
The Company has in place compliance programs and internal and external experts to help guide our business in complying with these and other existing laws and regulations that apply to us around the globe; and we have made, and plan to continue making, necessary expenditures for compliance with these laws and regulations. We also expect that our many suppliers, consultants and other third parties working on our behalf share our commitment to compliance, and we have policies and procedures in place to manage these relationships, though they inherently involve a lesser degree of control over operations and governance. We do not expect that the Company's expenditures for compliance with current government regulations, including current environmental regulations, will have a material effect on our total capital expenditures, earnings or competitive position in fiscal year 2025 as compared to prior periods.
Human Capital
. Our employees are a key source of competitive advantage. Their actions, guided by our Purpose, Values and Principles (PVPs), are critical to the long-term success of our business. We aim to retain our talented employees by offering competitive compensation and benefits, strong career development and a respectful and inclusive culture that provides equal opportunity for all.
Our Board of Directors, through the Compensation and Leadership Development Committee (C&LD Committee), provides oversight of the Company's policies and strategy relating to talent, including equality and inclusion, as well as the Company's compensation principles and practices. The C&LD Committee also evaluates and approves the Company's compensation plans, policies and programs applicable to our senior executives.
Employees
As of June 30, 2024, the Company had approximately 108,000 employees, unchanged versus the prior year. The total number of employees is an estimate of total Company employees excluding interns, co-ops, contractors and employees of joint ventures. 48% of our employees are in manufacturing roles and 28% of our employees are located in the United States. 42% of our global employees are women and 32% of our U.S. employees identify as multicultural.
Training and Development
We focus on attracting, developing and retaining the widest pool of talent available, both from universities and the broader market. We recruit from universities across markets in which we compete and are generally able to select from the top talent. We focus on developing our employees by providing a variety of job experiences, training programs and skill development opportunities. Given our develop-from-within model for staffing most of our senior leadership positions, it is particularly important for us to ensure holistic growth and full engagement of our employees.
Diversity, Equality and Inclusion
As a consumer products company, we believe that it is important for our workforce to reflect the diversity of our consumers worldwide. We also seek to foster an inclusive work environment where each individual can bring their authentic self, which helps drive innovation and enables us to better serve our consumers. We aspire to achieve equal gender representation globally and at key management and leadership levels. Within the U.S. workforce, our aspiration is to achieve 40% multicultural representation overall as well as at management and leadership levels.
Compensation and Benefits
Market-competitive compensation and reward programs are critical elements of our employee value equation to attract and retain the best talent. Our total rewards programs are based on the principles of paying for performance, paying competitively versus peer companies that we compete with for talent in the marketplace and focusing on long-term success through a combination of short-term and long-term incentive programs. We also offer competitive benefit programs, including retirement plans and health insurance, in line with local country practices, with flexibility to accommodate the needs of a diverse workforce.
Sustainability
. Environmental sustainability is integrated into our business strategy. We are focused on designing and manufacturing irresistibly superior products that are more sustainable. We aim to reduce our own environmental footprint and enable our consumers to reduce their footprint without compromising on the performance of the products they use. We develop and license technologies that can be used across industries to improve environmental sustainability at a broader scale.
The Procter & Gamble Company        3
Combined, this approach intends to positively impact the total environmental impact of the Company while driving market growth and value creation.
In 2021, the Company announced a 2040 net zero ambition. Our Climate Transition Action Plan outlines the Company's ongoing efforts toward reducing greenhouse gas emissions across scopes 1 and 2 and elements of scope 3. The Company has also declared objectives towards purchasing renewable electricity for our operations, reducing use of virgin petroleum-based plastic in packaging, increasing the recyclability or reusability of packaging, responsible sourcing of key forest-based commodities, improving efficiency of water usage in our operations and driving a global portfolio of water restoration projects that help address water scarcity in key water basins. Our progress towards these objectives may be influenced and impacted by various stakeholders and developments beyond our control.
Sustainability related disclosures included in this Annual Report, our Proxy Statement and our sustainability reports are informed by standards and guidelines such as the Global Reporting Initiative (GRI) and the Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures (TCFD). The "materiality" thresholds in those standards and guidelines may differ from the concept of "materiality" for purposes of the federal securities laws and disclosures required by the Commission's rules in this Annual Report. References to our sustainability reports and website are for informational purposes only and neither the sustainability reports nor the other information on our website is incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K. Additional detailed information on our sustainability efforts can be found on our website at https://pginvestor.com/esg.
PG.quantitative_report:
# Procter & Gamble (PG) — Quantitative Report
**Decision date: 2025-07-01 | Latest filing: 2025-03-31 (Q3 FY2025)**

---

## 1. Financial Trajectory

**Revenue:** Latest quarterly revenue of $19.78B represents a QoQ decline of -9.6% (from $21.88B) and a YoY decline of -2.1% (from $20.20B). TTM revenue stands at $83.93B. Quarterly revenue has oscillated between $20.20B and $21.88B over the trailing eight quarters, with Q3 fiscal periods consistently lighter (~$19.8–20.2B).

**Earnings:** Latest EPS diluted of $1.54, down -18.1% QoQ (from $1.88) but up +1.3% YoY (from $1.52). TTM net income totals $15.58B. EPS has ranged from $1.27 (Q4 FY2024) to $1.88 (Q2 FY2025) over the eight-quarter window.

**Margins:** Gross margin 51.3% (gross profit $10.08B / revenue $19.78B = 51.0% for the quarter). Operating margin 23.8% (reported) versus 22.5% calculated for the quarter ($4.56B / $19.78B). Net margin 18.6%. TTM operating income of $19.98B on $83.93B revenue yields 23.8%. Margins have been stable within a tight band: gross margins ranged 48.5%–52.4%, operating margins ranged 18.9%–26.7%, and net margins reported range 0.172–0.186.

**Cash flow:** Not available. All quarterly cash flow data points are missing.

**Balance sheet:** Total assets $122.98B, total liabilities $70.44B, total equity $52.55B. Equity has grown steadily from $47.06B (Q4 FY2023, 2023-06-30) to $52.55B, an increase of $5.49B (+11.7%) over eight quarters. Total assets have expanded modestly from $120.83B to $122.98B (+1.8%). Liabilities have declined from $73.76B to $70.44B (-4.5%). Debt-to-equity ratio: 1.34× ($70.44B / $52.55B).

---

## 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality:** Net margin of 0.186, gross margin of 0.513, and ROE of 0.297 are all stable across the reported valuation history (net margin ranged 0.172–0.186; ROE ranged 0.276–0.303). Consistent margin profiles suggest low earnings volatility. However, the YoY revenue decline of -2.1% offset by flat EPS (+1.3%) implies margin expansion offsetting top-line softness — worth monitoring for sustainability.

**Balance-sheet resilience:** Equity growth of +$5.49B over eight quarters with declining liabilities ($73.76B → $70.44B) shows deleveraging. Debt-to-equity of 1.34× is moderate. Total assets remain stable (~$120–126B range). **Cash flow generation is unobservable** — a material gap in assessing true balance-sheet resilience and capital return capacity.

---

## 3. Valuation vs. Own History

| Metric | Latest (2025-06-30) | 12-Month Range | Position in Range |
|---|---|---|---|
| P/E diluted | 25.05× | 24.8× – 30.8× | **Near bottom (7% from trough)** |
| P/B | 7.43× | 7.4× – 8.7× | **At bottom of range** |
| P/S | 4.65× | n/a | n/a |
| Earnings yield | 4.0% | n/a | n/a |

P/E has compressed from 29.49× (2024-12-10) to 25.05×, a contraction of -15.0%. P/B has compressed from 8.60× (2024-09-04) to 7.43×, a contraction of -13.6%. Both metrics sit at or near their 12-month lows despite stable margins and ROE. The valuation contraction has occurred alongside relatively stable fundamentals (net margin 0.186, ROE 0.297 currently vs. 0.172/0.276 at troughs in late 2024).

---

## 4. Price Level & Return Context

**Latest price:** $159.32 (2025-06-30).

**52-week range:** Low $156.58, high $180.43. Current price is **-11.7% from peak** and **+1.8% from trough**.

**Quarterly return:** -5.2% (from $168.03 on 2025-03-28).

**Annual return:** -4.9% (from $167.45 on 2024-06-26).

**Quarterly path:**
- 2024-06-26 → 2024-09-25: +2.9% ($167.45 → $172.26)
- 2024-09-25 → 2024-12-24: -1.9% ($172.26 → $168.94)
- 2024-12-24 → 2025-03-28: -0.5% ($168.94 → $168.03)
- 2025-03-28 → 2025-06-30: -5.2% ($168.03 → $159.32)

Price weakness accelerated in the most recent quarter (-5.2%) against a backdrop of weak Q3 results (revenue -9.6% QoQ, EPS -18.1% QoQ). The stock has delivered negative total returns over both one quarter and one year despite stable long-term fundamentals.

---

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside:**
- P/E of 25.05× at the bottom of its 12-month range (24.8–30.8×); reversion to the 12-month midpoint of ~27.8× would imply ~11% re-rating on current TTM EPS of ~$6.30 (sum of last four quarters: $1.54 + $1.88 + $1.61 + $1.27 = $6.30), yielding an implied price of ~$175, representing +9.8% upside.
- Stable margins (gross 51.3%, operating 23.8%, net 18.6%) and ROE of 29.7% across the valuation history, suggesting fundamental durability.
- Equity growth of +11.7% over eight quarters with declining liabilities indicates improving balance-sheet health.

**Downside:**
- QoQ revenue decline of -9.6% and EPS decline of -18.1% in the latest quarter signal potential fundamental softening.
- Price is +1.8% above the 52-week low of $156.58; further deterioration in Q4 results could test new lows.
- P/B at 7.43× is at the absolute floor of the 12-month range; further multiple compression is possible.
- Cash flow data entirely absent — inability to verify cash conversion or dividend sustainability.
- Annual return of -4.9% with four consecutive quarters of declining/flat price momentum.

---

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

PG trades at the bottom of its 12-month valuation range (P/E 25.05× vs. 24.8–30.8×; P/B 7.43× vs. 7.4–8.7×) with stable margin and ROE metrics. The -5.2% quarterly price decline and -4.9% annual return have compressed multiples despite consistent profitability (net margin 0.186, ROE 0.297). A mean-reversion to the P/E midpoint of ~27.8× on TTM EPS of $6.30 implies ~10% upside to ~$175.

However, the latest quarter exhibited meaningful QoQ weakness (revenue -9.6%, EPS -18.1%), and the stock sits only 1.8% above its 52-week low. The complete absence of cash flow data prevents assessment of free cash flow yield, cash conversion, and dividend coverage — a material blind spot for a dividend-focused consumer staples name.

**Provisional conclusion:** The quantitative setup offers asymmetric risk/reward modestly skewed to the upside, contingent on Q4 revenue/EPS stabilization. Valuation multiple expansion potential from trough levels (~+10% to midpoint) outweighs further downside risk to the next support zone ($156.58, -1.7%), but the missing cash flow evidence and recent negative earnings momentum warrant caution in conviction.
PG.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: The Procter & Gamble Company (PG)**
**Decision Date: 2025-07-01 | Evidence Cutoff: Pre-2025-07-01**

### 1. Business / Franchise Read from Narrative
P&G is a world-leading multinational consumer goods company (10-K, 2024-08-05). It focuses on providing trusted, branded daily-use products to improve consumers' lives, with sales in ~180 countries. Its business model is built on delivering sustainable, balanced top- and bottom-line growth through creating, manufacturing, and marketing a diversified portfolio. The company states its success depends on driving "irresistible superiority" across product, packaging, communication, retail execution, and value. It operates in highly competitive markets but often holds leadership or significant shares, competing against global brands and private labels. P&G emphasizes strong trademarks, patents, and a "develop-from-within" talent model. Key customer concentration exists, with Walmart accounting for approximately 16% of 2024 sales.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated Evidence)
*   **Outlook & Guidance:** As of May 18, 2025, P&G raised its sales outlook, stating demand was stabilizing, with quarterly results outpacing expectations and indicating a contained US economic slowdown (web context, 2025-05-18). As of June 5-6, 2025, the company's outlook for 2025 forecasts 2–4% sales growth and 10–12% diluted EPS growth (web context, 2025-06-05, 2025-06-06). Margin resilience is noted, with 2–4% core EPS growth guidance cited as achievable given cash flow stability (web context, 2025-06-06).
*   **Product & Innovation:** A SWOT analysis dated May 28, 2025, highlights P&G's focus on product innovation as a key opportunity, citing recent launches including SK-II LXP in China and Luvs Platinum Protection (web context, 2025-05-28).
*   **Corporate Actions / Strategy:** Articles from June 2025 describe P&G in a "Restructuring Crossroads," needing to balance cost cuts with strategic reinvestment. Its 2025 outlook depends on executing priorities like defending high-value categories and managing restructuring (web context, 2025-06-05).
*   **Market Opportunities:** Market reports from May 25-30, 2025, outline growth opportunities in Saudi Arabia for home fragrances and hair care, driven by disposable incomes and shifts toward natural/organic products (company news, 2025-05-25, 2025-05-27, 2025-05-30).
*   **Dividend Profile:** As of June 27, 2025, P&G is identified as offering a 2.8% dividend yield and a track record of consistent growth, framing it as a predictable defensive holding (web context, 2025-06-27). Historical context on Dividend Kings (50+ years of dividend raises) was provided on May 25, 2025 (company news, 2025-05-25).

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets
**Risks** (primarily from web context [risk]):
*   **Litigation & Legal:** Multiple lawsuits are referenced, including a shareholder proposal (June 10, 2025) alleging DEI initiatives create regulatory and litigation risk for DEI-related disclosures and compensation (web context, 2025-06-10). Lawsuits (Giarrizzo, Dean v. P&G, May 28, 2025) criticize environmental marketing ("Restore" promise) as potentially misleading or insufficient, covering less than 1% of logged areas (web context, 2025-05-28). A class action related to benzene in antiperspirant sprays (Old Spice, Secret) is noted, with Valisure filing an FDA petition (web context, 2025-05-28).
*   **Regulatory & Reputational:** The shareholder proposal explicitly states P&G faces "regulatory, reputational and litigation risk" from its DEI policies (web context, 2025-06-10). Broader environmental and sustainability claims face scrutiny, as indicated by lawsuits alleging "token gesture" efforts (web context, 2025-05-28).
*   **Competition & Input Costs:** The Saudi market analysis notes challenges from volatile raw material costs (company news, 2025-05-30). Competition is described as "highly competitive" with private label and global rivals (10-K, 2024-08-05).

**Offsets** (from supplied text):
*   The company's strong, defensible market positions (37% share in Personal & Household Products noted in web context, 2025-06-27) and dominant brands provide resilience.
*   Current operational outlook is positive, with stabilized demand and raised sales guidance (web context, 2025-05-18). Management asserts execution on innovation and cost productivity can support growth (web context, 2025-06-05).
*   P&G states it has compliance programs and experts for regulations and does not expect regulatory compliance costs to materially impact FY2025 earnings (10-K, 2024-08-05).
*   The "Dividend King" status and historical payout stability are highlighted as attractive attributes for income investors (company news, 2025-05-25; web context, 2025-06-27).

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Neutral.** The evidence presents a **strong, stable franchise** with a dominant market position, consistent execution, and a positive near-term operational outlook as of mid-2025. The company's guidance for sales and EPS growth is confirmed across multiple sources. However, the **material and multi-faceted legal and reputational risks** documented in recent news—spanning environmental marketing, product safety, and DEI-related policy disclosures—present significant qualitative headwinds. While current financial guidance offsets some concern, these risks introduce uncertainty regarding regulatory actions, litigation costs, and brand reputation that could impact long-term value. The assessment balances the confirmed protective qualities of the brand portfolio and dividend track record against the confirmed salience of escalating legal and scrutiny challenges.

corrections.issue: The quant report states 'Gross margin 51.3% (gross profit $10.08B / revenue $19.78B = 51.0% for the quarter)' — the raw pack confirms gross_margin=0.513 and the arithmetic yields 51.0%, so the 51.3% figure is the reported ratio from the valuation panel while 51.0% is the calculated quarterly figure. The report conflates the two without clarifying which period each refers to. Minor inconsistency but not directionally material.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; the discrepancy is cosmetic and both figures appear in the raw pack. The report should note that 51.3% is the latest valuation-panel figure (which may reflect a trailing or adjusted basis) versus the 51.0% calculated from Q3 income statement data.

corrections.issue: The quant report calculates TTM EPS as $1.54 + $1.88 + $1.61 + $1.27 = $6.30 and uses this for the mean-reversion price target of ~$175. The raw pack confirms these four quarterly EPS figures sum to $6.30. However, the report also states 'TTM net income totals $15.58B' — dividing $15.58B by the implied ~2.47B shares yields ~$6.30 EPS, which is internally consistent. No issue here; verified.
corrections.required_action: No action needed; calculation is consistent with raw data.

corrections.issue: The qual report cites '2–4% core EPS growth guidance' from a June 6, 2025 web source, and separately '10–12% diluted EPS growth' from a June 5, 2025 web source. These are two different EPS growth metrics (core vs. diluted) presented in close proximity without clear distinction, which could confuse the reader about the actual guidance range. The raw evidence confirms both figures appear in separate web context entries.
corrections.required_action: The qual report should more clearly distinguish between the 2–4% core EPS growth and 10–12% diluted EPS growth figures, noting they are different metrics from different sources, to avoid the impression of contradictory guidance.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: PG trades at the bottom of its 12-month valuation range (P/E 25.05x vs. 24.8-30.8x; P/B 7.43x vs. 7.4-8.7x) with stable margins and ROE, but recent QoQ weakness (revenue -9.6%, EPS -18.1%) and a -4.9% annual return temper conviction. The setup offers modest asymmetric upside (~10% to P/E midpoint reversion) against limited downside to the 52-week low, but missing cash flow data and escalating legal headwinds warrant a neutral stance.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: PG trades at the bottom of its 12-month valuation range (P/E 25.05x vs. 24.8-30.8x; P/B 7.43x vs. 7.4-8.7x) with stable margins and ROE, but recent QoQ weakness (revenue -9.6%, EPS -18.1%) and a -4.9% annual return temper conviction. The setup offers modest asymmetric upside (~10% to P/E midpoint reversion) against limited downside to the 52-week low, but missing cash flow data and escalating legal headwinds warrant a neutral stance.
key_factors: Valuation at 12-month trough: P/E of 25.05x sits 7% above its 12-month low (24.8x) and P/B of 7.43x is at the absolute floor of its range (7.4-8.7x), with both metrics having compressed 13-15% over the past year despite stable fundamentals (net margin 0.186, ROE 0.297).

Stable profitability metrics: Gross margin 51.3%, operating margin 23.8%, and ROE 29.7% have remained within tight bands across the trailing eight quarters, with equity growing +11.7% ($47.06B to $52.55B) and liabilities declining -4.5% ($73.76B to $70.44B).

Recent fundamental softening: Q3 FY2025 showed QoQ revenue decline of -9.6% ($21.88B to $19.78B) and EPS decline of -18.1% ($1.88 to $1.54), with the stock price down -5.2% in the latest quarter and -4.9% annually, sitting just 1.8% above the 52-week low of $156.58.

Guidance and legal overhang: Management raised sales outlook on May 18, 2025 citing stabilizing demand with 2-4% sales growth and 10-12% diluted EPS growth guidance, but multiple active lawsuits (benzene in antiperspirants, environmental marketing claims, DEI-related shareholder proposals) introduce reputational and regulatory uncertainty.

Latest 10-K business section (newest first):
  filing_date=2025-03-05 section=business period_end=2025-01-31
ITEM 1. BUSINESS
Overview
Salesforce, Inc. ("Salesforce," the "Company," "we" or "our") is a global leader in customer relationship management ("CRM") technology, enabling companies of every size and industry to connect with their customers through the power of data, artificial intelligence ("AI"), CRM and trust. Founded in 1999, we bring humans together with AI agents to drive customer success on one deeply unified platform.
Our AI-powered Salesforce P
…[truncated 21560 chars]…
tunity, provides a pathway to effectively monetize the acquired products and drive significant operational efficiencies and presents a clear timeline for value accretion. Our acquisitions can range in size and complexity, from those that enhance or complement existing products and accelerate development of features to large-scale acquisitions that result in new service offerings. Our goal is to prioritize the use of our balance sheet, through cash and debt, to complete acquisitions.
We also manage a portfolio of strategic investments in both privately held and publicly traded companies focused primarily on enterprise cloud companies, technology startups and SIs. Our investments range from early to late stage companies, including investments made concurrent with a company's initial public offering. We invest in companies that we believe are digitally transforming their industries, improving customer experiences, helping us expand our solution ecosystem or supporting other corporate initiatives, including AI. We plan to continue making these types of strategic investments as opportunities arise that we find attractive, including investments in companies representing targeted businesses, technological initiatives and geographies. Our strategy includes growing our strategic investment portfolio, in part, by reinvesting proceeds from the sales of strategic investments.
7
Table of Contents
Technology, Development and Operations
We primarily deliver our solutions as highly scalable cloud computing application and platform services on a multi-tenant technology architecture. Multi-tenancy is an architectural approach that allows us to operate a single application instance for multiple organizations, treating all customers as separate tenants who run in virtual isolation from each other. This approach allows us to spread the cost of delivering our services across our user base and scale our business faster than traditional software vendors while focusing our resources on building new functionality and enhancing existing offerings.
We provide our services through cloud computing platform partners who offer Infrastructure-as-a-Service, including servers, storage, databases and networking, as well as through infrastructure designed and operated by us but secured within third-party data center facilities. We continue to invest and expand the deployment of Hyperforce, which allows our platform and applications to be delivered rapidly and reliably to locations worldwide and provides our customers autonomy and control over data residency.
Our technology and product efforts are focused on improving and enhancing the features, functionality, performance, availability and security of our existing service offerings, as well as developing new features, functionality and services. We also remain focused on integrating businesses, services and technologies from acquisitions. Performance, functional depth, security, usability, ease of integration and configuration and sustainability of our solutions influence our technology decisions and product direction.
Competition
The market for our service offerings is highly competitive, rapidly evolving and fragmented, and subject to changing technology with low barriers to entry, shifting customer needs and frequent introductions of new products and services.
Our current competitors include:
- vendors of packaged business software, as well as companies offering enterprise applications delivered through on-premises offerings from enterprise software application vendors and cloud computing application service providers, either individually or with others;
- software companies that provide their product or service free of charge as a single product or when bundled with other offerings, or only charge a premium for advanced features and functionality, as well as companies that offer solutions that are sold without a direct sales organization;
- vendors who offer software tailored to specific services, industries or market segments, as opposed to our full suite of service offerings including suppliers of traditional business intelligence and data preparation products, integration software vendors, marketing vendors, e-commerce solutions vendors, or AI software and service vendors;
- productivity tool and email providers, unified communications providers and consumer application companies that have entered the business software market; and
- traditional platform development environment companies and cloud computing development platform companies who may develop toolsets and products that allow customers to build new applications, including AI augmented applications, that run on the customers' current infrastructure or as hosted services, as well as would-be customers who may develop enterprise applications for internal use.
We believe more companies may become competitive threats due to the attractiveness of the markets in which we operate. We also expect our competition to change and evolve as we expand into more markets, with new offerings.
Customers
We sell to businesses of all sizes and in almost every industry worldwide. The number of paying subscriptions at each of our customers ranges from one to hundreds of thousands. None of our customers accounted for more than ten percent of our revenues in fiscal years 2025, 2024 or 2023. In addition, we do not have any material dependencies on any specific product, service or particular group or groups.
Customer Service and Support
We offer professional services to help customers achieve business results faster with Salesforce solutions. Our architects and innovation program teams act as advisors to plan and execute digital transformations for our customers. This includes implementation services for multi-offering and complex deployments. We provide best-practices and AI-based recommendations and adoption programs globally. In addition, we provide advanced education, including in-person and online courses, to certify our customers and partners on architecting, administering, deploying and developing our service offerings.
Our global customer support group responds to both business and technical inquiries about the use of our products via the web, telephone, email, social networks and other channels. We provide standard customer support during regular business hours to customers as part of our paying subscription editions. We also offer premier customer support that is either included in a premium success offering or sold for an additional fee, which can include services such as priority access to technical resources, developer support and system administration. In addition, we offer a premier priority support add-on that includes proactive monitoring, rapid incident response and instruction from a dedicated support team knowledgeable about the customer's specific
8
Table of Contents
enterprise architecture. With the launch of Agentforce, we've made it easier for customers to find answers by providing an even more intuitive experience on our Salesforce Help page. Agentforce can resolve customer issues swiftly and accurately because answers are grounded in our trusted unstructured content unified in Data Cloud. By automating simple and repetitive tasks, our support engineers will be able to augment and scale their time in a more valuable way.
Sales and Marketing
We sell our services primarily through our direct sales force, which comprises sales personnel based in regional hubs, field sales personnel based in territories close to their customers and self-service offerings.
To a lesser extent, we also utilize a network of partners who refer sales leads to us and assist in selling to prospects. This network includes global consulting firms, SIs and other partners. In return, we typically pay these partners a fee based on the first-year subscription revenue generated by the customers they refer. We continue to invest in developing additional distributi

CRM.quantitative_report:
**Quantitative Analysis Report: CRM as of 2025-07-01**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue & Earnings:** Q1 2025 (April 30) revenue was $9.83B, a QoQ decline of -1.6% from Q4 2024's $9.99B. Net income was $1.54B, with diluted EPS of $1.59, down -9.7% QoQ from $1.76. TTM revenue (sum of last 4 quarters) was $38.59B with TTM net income of $6.21B.
- **Margins:** Q1 2025 gross margin was 76.9% ($7.56B / $9.83B), operating margin was 19.7% ($1.94B / $9.83B), and net margin was 15.7% ($1.54B / $9.83B). The TTM operating margin was 19.3% ($7.44B / $38.59B). Margins have generally expanded over the last seven quarters, with net margin rising from 14.8% in Q3 2023 to 17.1% in Q4 2024 before the Q1 2025 contraction.
- **Cash Flow:** Quarterly free cash flow data is unavailable. This prevents direct cash generation analysis.
- **Balance Sheet:** Total equity was $60.67B, total debt was $8.44B, yielding a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.14. Equity grew from $58.08B in Q3 2023 to the current level. Total assets decreased from $102.93B in Q4 2024 to $98.61B in Q1 2025.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** The net margin improvement over seven quarters (from ~14.8% to a peak of 17.1%) suggests improving operational leverage. The Q1 2025 margin contraction and EPS decline are notable. Without cash flow statements, the alignment of earnings with cash cannot be assessed.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** The balance sheet is strong with a low leverage ratio (debt/equity = 0.14). Equity is substantial at $60.67B versus $8.44B in debt. The debt level has decreased from $9.43B a year prior. Asset base fluctuations appear tied to operational cycles.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- Valuation ratios have compressed over the last year. The current PE (diluted) of 42.88 is near the low end of its trailing window range (37.1 to 77.7). Price-to-book (4.34) and price-to-sales (7.01) are similarly at the lower ends of their respective ranges (P/B: 3.8–6.1). The current earnings yield is 2.3%. Net margin and ROE have improved to the upper ends of their recent ranges (0.164, 0.101).

**4. Price Context vs. Fundamentals**
- The closing price on 2025-06-30 was $272.69. The 52-week high was $369.00, low was $230.00.
- **Quarterly Return:** +1.0% (from $269.97 on 2025-03-28).
- **Annual Return:** +12.1% (from $243.15 on 2024-06-26).
- The price experienced significant volatility: a +25.7% increase in the quarter ending 2024-12-24, followed by a -21.6% decline in the subsequent quarter, and a modest +1.0% gain in the latest quarter. The stock's annual return trails the S&P 500's... (implied by the lack of benchmark data; Mark unavailable for explicit benchmark comparison). The price is trading below its 52-week high, having pulled back from overvalued levels (PE ~73 in Dec 2025) to a historically lower valuation (PE ~43).

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** Valuation multiples have compressed significantly from historical highs. If the PE reverts toward the midpoint of its historical range (e.g., toward 55-60) while TTM earnings hold, potential price appreciation is quantifiable. The strong, low-leverage balance sheet provides financial flexibility. Margin expansion over the longer trajectory demonstrates earnings power.
- **Downside:** The most recent quarter shows a QoQ decline in revenue (-1.6%) and earnings (-9.7%). If this signals a growth normalization, it could pressure valuations further toward the low end of the PE range (37.1). The absence of cash flow data is a material information gap, precluding assessment of cash conversion quality. Annual price volatility is high.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
- At a PE of 42.9, the stock is priced at the low end of its recent valuation history. The company has demonstrated revenue growth and margin expansion over the past two years, supported by a resilient balance sheet. However, the most recent quarter displayed decelerating revenue and earnings trends.
- The risk is that the Q1 2025 performance signals a fundamental slowdown, which could push the valuation toward its lower historical bound (PE 37.1) given the absence of supportive cash flow data. The reward is a reversion toward mean valuation if earnings stabilize and margins hold.
- The quantitative risk/reward appears balanced but leans cautiously positive, contingent on the next quarter's data confirming whether Q1 2025 was a temporary dip or a trend reversal. The low leverage provides a margin of safety, but the lack of cash flow visibility limits the quality assessment.
CRM.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Salesforce (CRM)**
**Decision Date: 2025-07-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read (from 10-K, 2025-03-05)**
Salesforce is a global leader in customer relationship management (CRM) technology. Its core franchise is built on a deeply unified, AI-powered platform that connects customer data to create a single source of truth. The business is organized around key service offerings: Sales, Service, Marketing, Commerce, Slack, and Platform (including MuleSoft for integration and Tableau for analytics). A central strategic pillar is **Agentforce**, an agentic layer for deploying autonomous AI agents across business functions, and **Data Cloud**, a hyperscale data engine. The company sells primarily on a subscription basis to businesses of all sizes globally, with no single customer representing more than 10% of revenue. Its growth strategy focuses on cross-selling to existing customers, expanding geographically, developing industry-specific solutions, and leveraging its partner ecosystem.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated Evidence)**
*   **Financial Guidance & Performance (2025-05-28):** Salesforce raised its revenue and adjusted profit forecasts for fiscal 2026, citing robust demand for cloud services and accelerating AI adoption. This followed Q1 results that exceeded expectations, with 7.6% revenue growth and a 12% increase in current remaining performance obligation (cRPO).
*   **AI Product & Market Positioning (2025-06-05, 2025-06-22, 2025-06-24):** Multiple financial articles identify Salesforce as a key beneficiary of the AI trend, specifically citing its **Agentforce** solution as a growth catalyst. The company is frequently mentioned alongside other major tech firms in the context of the expanding AI market.
*   **Competitive & Market Environment (2025-06-03, 2025-06-18):** A BofA survey indicates data analytics remains a top software spending priority, which benefits Salesforce's platform. However, the broader M&A environment is noted as cautious, with a lack of megamergers due to economic and policy uncertainty.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Security & Data Breach (2025-06-04):** A report indicates hackers abused a modified Salesforce-related app to steal data and extort companies. This highlights platform security and third-party app ecosystem risks.
    *   *Offset:* The company's 10-K emphasizes "trust" as a core value and foundation, with a focus on enterprise-grade security, privacy, and compliance. The platform includes a "Trust Layer" for AI guardrails.
*   **Risk: Regulatory & Compliance (2025-06-26):** A Wikipedia entry notes Salesforce Japan failed to meet a legal disability employment quota from 2009-2021 (excluding 2017), indicating potential operational compliance risks in specific jurisdictions.
    *   *Offset:* The 10-K states the company's values are "grounded in legal and regulatory frameworks" and that it is "dedicated to fostering a workplace that complies with these protections."
*   **Risk: Intense Competition (2025-03-05):** The 10-K describes the market as "highly competitive, rapidly evolving and fragmented" with low barriers to entry, including threats from free software models, specialized vendors, and platform developers.
    *   *Offset:* The company's strategy emphasizes its "deeply unified platform," cross-selling, and industry-specific solutions to create a competitive moat.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Positive.** The supplied evidence presents a franchise with a clear, integrated AI-centric strategy (Agentforce, Data Cloud) that is resonating in the market, as confirmed by recent upward financial guidance. The company is consistently cited as a leader in the high-growth AI software space. While material risks exist around security, compliance, and intense competition, the narrative from the company's own filings and recent market commentary suggests these are being actively managed and are offset by the platform's strategic positioning and strong customer adoption trends. The qualitative narrative is one of a market leader successfully aligning its product suite with a major technological shift.

They appear to be computed from quarterly income statements (e.g., Q3 2023: 1.22B/8.72B=14.0%; Q4 2024: 1.71B/9.99B=17.1%). The Q3 2023 figure of 14.8% does not match the computed 14.0%. This is a minor numerical inconsistency. → Verify and correct the Q3 2023 net margin figure; computed from raw data it is approximately 14.0%, not 14.8%.

**Unsupported claims:**
- The quant report's claim of 'PE ~73 in Dec 2025' references a future date not present in the evidence; the raw pack shows pe_diluted=73.16 on 2024-12-10.
- The quant report states 'The stock's annual return trails the S&P 500's...' but no S&P 500 benchmark data is provided in the raw packs; this comparison is unsupported.

ITEM 1.    Business
OVERVIEW
Visa is one of the world's leaders in digital payments. Our purpose is to uplift everyone, e

ted its acquisition of Pismo, a global, cloud-native issuer processing and core banking platform with operations in Latin America, Asia Pacific and Europe. Pismo's technology platform expands Visa's offerings to include core banking and card-issuer processing capabilities across credit, debit and prepaid cards via cloud native APIs and also helps enable Visa to provide support and connectivity for emerging payment frameworks and real-time payments (RTP) networks for financial institution clients.
We provide a range of other services and digital solutions to issuers, such as account controls, digital issuance and branded consumer experiences. In addition, Visa offers loyalty and benefits solutions to issuers aimed at creating compelling and differentiated cardholder experiences, as well as Buy Now, Pay Later (BNPL) capabilities, which allow shoppers the flexibility to pay for a purchase in equal payments over a defined period of time. Visa is investing in installments as a payments strategy — by offering Visa credentials and BNPL solutions to issuers and fintechs.
Acceptance Solutions
Visa Acceptance Solutions provide modular, value-added services in addition to the traditional gateway function of connecting merchants to payment processing. Using the Visa Acceptance Platform, acquirers, payment service providers, independent software vendors and merchants can improve the way their consumers engage and transact; help to mitigate fraud and lower operational costs; and adapt to changing business requirements. They can also connect with other fintechs through a global payment management platform to use their services. Using an omnichannel solution with a cloud-based architecture, Visa Acceptance Solutions capabilities, which includes Cybersource and Authorize.net, provide new and enhanced payment integrations with ecommerce platforms, enabling sellers and acquirers to provide tailored commerce experiences with payments seamlessly embedded.
In addition, Visa Acceptance Solutions provide secure, reliable services for merchants and acquirers that reduce friction and drive acceptance. Examples include Token Management Service, which helps simplify network token adoption, access and management for merchant and acquiring clients, provides a single integration point into major card networks and is used standalone or easily integrated into other payment solutions (please see the Tokenization discussion above); Global Urban Mobility, which supports transit operators to accept Visa contactless payments in addition to closed-loop payment solutions; and Account Updater, which provides updated account information for merchants to help strengthen customer relationships and retention. Visa also offers Dispute

Management Services, including a network-agnostic solution from Verifi that enables merchants to prevent and resolve disputes with a single connection.
Risk and Identity Solutions
Visa's Risk and Identity Solutions transform data into insights for near real-time decisions and facilitate account holder authentication to help financial institution and merchant clients prevent fraud and protect account holder data. With the increasing popularity of omnichannel commerce and digital payments among consumers, fraud prevention helps increase trust in digital payments. The Visa Protect suite of solutions include a range of products that empower financial institutions and merchants with tools that facilitate automation, simplify fraud prevention and enhance payment security, such as: Visa Consumer Authentication Service, Visa Protect Authentication Intelligence, and Visa Provisioning Intelligence. These offerings highlight our artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning capabilities, for example, Visa Protect Authentication Intelligence uses machine learning algorithms to identify fraud for Visa Secure authentication requests and Visa Provisioning Intelligence is an AI-based product designed to prevent tokens from being fraudulently provisioned.
In March 2024, Visa announced three new products as part of the end-to-end Visa Protect suite that are designed to reduce fraud across immediate A2A and card not present (CNP) payments, as well as transactions both on and off Visa's network. Visa Deep Authorization is an AI-powered transaction risk scoring solution tailored to better manage CNP payments, which strengthens the protection of Visa's transactions through transaction risk scoring. Visa Protect for A2A Payments is our first fraud prevention solution built specifically for real-time non-card payments. Finally, Visa Risk Manager with scheme agnostic Visa Advanced Authorization is a comprehensive AI-powered fraud risk management solution. Visa's value-added fraud prevention tools layer on top of a suite of our network programs that protect the safety and integrity of the payment ecosystem, help to prevent, detect and mitigate threats.
Open Banking Solutions
Since our acquisition of Tink AB, an open banking platform, we continue to accelerate the development and adoption of open banking securely and at scale. Visa's open banking capabilities range from data access use cases, such as account verification, balance check and personal finance management, to payment initiation capabilities, such as A2A transactions and merchant payments. These capabilities can help our partner businesses deliver valuable services to their customers.
In fiscal 2024, we expanded our presence in Europe and began expanding into the U.S. with a suite of product offerings that enable users to connect accounts and provide trusted parties with access to their financial data, including digitizing and streamlining the A2A payments experience. In fiscal 2025, we plan to launch Visa A2A in the UK, an open system bringing standards, rules and a dispute management service to eligible banks and businesses, enabling them to provide consumers with a more streamlined bill payment experience. Key features will include increased protection, more optionality by allowing consumers to pay directly from their bank accounts and enhanced controls over payment permissions.
Advisory Services
Visa Advisory Services offers deep payments expertise through a global consulting practice, proprietary analytics models, data scientists and economists, marketing services and managed services to deliver insights for issuers, acquirers, merchants, fintechs and other partners that help them make better business decisions and scale their operations.
Operating as the payments consulting arm of Visa, Visa Consulting and Analytics (VCA) utilizes our payments expertise and economic intelligence to identify actionable insights, make recommendations and help implement solutions. VCA is comprised of specialized advisory practices such as: strategy and commercial money movement, portfolio optimization, digital, AI, risk and implementation support, which drive measurable outcomes for our clients. VCA Managed Services embeds teams within client organizations to help execute key initiatives.
Visa Marketing Services provides clients with unique activation opportunities with Visa's sponsorships and utilizes our data analytics and understanding of customers' transactional behavior to provide marketing solutions designed to deliver effective results, drive brand preference and influence consumer behavior.

V.quantitative_report:
## Quantitative Report: Visa (V) — Decision Date 2025-07-01

---

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue** has trended upward across the eight-quarter window: $8.12B (2023-06) → $9.59B (2025-03). TTM revenue is $37.62B. Year-over-year growth for Q2 2025 is +9.3% ($9.59B vs. $8.78B). However, sequential growth decelerated to +0.9% ($9.59B vs. $9.51B), following a +7.0% QoQ step-up in Q3 2024 ($8.90B vs. $8.61B).

**Earnings are diverging from revenue.** TTM net income is $19.89B and TTM operating income is $23.96B. However, the latest quarter shows net income of $4.58B declining both YoY (−1.8% vs. $4.66B) and QoQ (−10.6% vs. $5.12B), despite revenue growth in both comparisons.

**Margins are compressing sharply in the latest quarter:**

| Quarter | Operating Margin (OpInc/Rev) | Net Margin (NI/Rev) |
|---|---|---|
| 2023-06 | 61.8% | 51.2% |
| 2023-09 | 64.5% | 54.4% |
| 2023-12 | 68.9% | 56.7% |
| 2024-03 | 60.9% | 53.1% |
| 2024-06 | 66.7% | 54.7% |
| 2024-09 | 66.0% | 55.3% |
| 2024-12 | 65.5% | 53.8% |
| **2025-03** | **56.6%** | **47.8%** |

The Q2 2025 operating margin of 56.6% and net margin of 47.8% are the lowest in the entire eight-quarter series. The TTM operating margin remains 63.7% ($23.96B / $37.62B), but the trajectory is deteriorating.

**Balance sheet** shows total equity declining from a peak of $40.48B (2024-03) to $38.03B (2025-03), a cumulative erosion of −6.0%. Total liabilities have risen from $51.91B to $54.82B over the same period. The latest debt-to-equity ratio is 1.44x ($54.82B / $38.03B). ROE remains exceptionally high at 52.3%, supported by the asset-light model and ongoing capital return.

**Cash flow data is unavailable** for all reported periods.

---

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality concern:** Revenue grew +9.3% YoY while net income fell −1.8% YoY in the latest quarter. Operating income declined from $5.35B (Q2 2024) to $5.43B (Q2 2025), only +1.5% against +9.3% revenue growth. This implies a significant cost ramp — operating expenses absorbed essentially all incremental revenue. The QoQ picture is worse: operating income fell −12.8% ($6.23B → $5.43B) on flat revenue. The source of this cost increase is not identifiable from the numbers alone.

**Balance-sheet resilience** is moderate. Equity of $38.03B provides a meaningful cushion, and the 52.3% ROE signals efficient capital deployment. However, the steady decline in equity (likely driven by buybacks exceeding net income retention) reduces the margin of safety over time. Total liabilities-to-equity of 1.44x is manageable but not conservative.

**Free cash flow and gross profit data are unavailable**, preventing further quality assessment. The absence of cash-flow data means operating accruals cannot be measured.

---

### 3. Valuation Versus Own History

**No valuation multiples are available** — PE diluted, EV/EBIT, FCF yield, and P/B are all marked n/a for the entire lookback window. The only valuation-relevant ratios supplied are profitability ratios:

- Net margin history: ranged from 0.50 to 0.55 over the trailing year; latest reading is 0.529, at the low end of the range.
- ROE history: ranged from 0.454 (2024-07-01) to 0.523 (latest), showing a steady upward drift driven by declining equity base rather than expanding profits.

Without PE, EV/EBIT, or P/B, **valuation cannot be assessed against the company's own history or against any multiple-based benchmark.** The only directional signal is that the earnings power supporting any given price level is weakening (margin compression + flat-to-declining quarterly earnings).

---

### 4. Price Level and Return Context

**Latest close:** $355.05 (2025-06-30).
**52-week range:** $252.70 – $375.51. Current price is at the 83rd percentile of this range, approximately 5.8% below the high.

**Return trajectory:**

| Period Start | Price | Delta to Next |
|---|---|---|
| 2024-06-26 | $273.60 | −1.5% |
| 2024-09-25 | $269.63 | +18.9% |
| 2024-12-24 | $320.65 | +6.9% |
| 2025-03-28 | $342.85 | +3.6% |

Annual return is +29.8%. Quarterly return has decelerated from +18.9% → +6.9% → +3.6%. The price recovery from the September 2024 low of $269.63 to the current $355.05 represents a +31.7% advance, but momentum is clearly fading.

**Fundamental-price tension:** The stock has appreciated ~30% over the past year while quarterly net income has declined (−1.8% YoY, −10.6% QoQ) and margins have compressed to the lowest level in the series. The price advance has outpaced the fundamental trajectory, creating a widening gap between valuation and near-term earnings power.

---

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside

**Upside factors (from the numbers):**
- Revenue growth remains solid at +9.3% YoY, with TTM revenue of $37.62B showing no deceleration in the top line.
- ROE of 52.3% and TTM net income of $19.89B confirm the business generates exceptional returns on capital.
- If the margin compression in Q2 2025 is temporary/reversible, a reversion to the ~65% operating margin range would imply quarterly operating income of ~$6.2B vs. the reported $5.43B, a +14% earnings recovery.

**Downside factors (from the numbers):**
- The margin deterioration is severe: operating margin fell from 65.5% (Q1 2025) to 56.6% (Q2 2025), an 890 basis-point decline in one quarter. If sustained, TTM operating income would fall well below $23.96B.
- Net income has declined in two of the last three quarters on a YoY basis (Q2 2025: −1.8%; Q3 2024 is not directly comparable to the prior-year series shown, but Q4 2024 at $5.32B is below Q4 2023's implied run-rate).
- Equity erosion of $2.45B over five quarters reduces balance-sheet resilience against a sustained earnings downturn.
- The stock is near its 52-week high (+29.8% annual return) while fundamental momentum (earnings, margins) is deteriorating. The price-to-fundamentals gap creates downside risk if margins do not recover.

---

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

The quantitative profile presents a **divergence between top-line strength and bottom-line deterioration**. Revenue grew +9.3% YoY, but the latest quarter delivered the lowest operating margin (56.6%) and net margin (47.8%) in the eight-quarter series, with net income declining both YoY (−1.8%) and QoQ (−10.6%). The stock has advanced +29.8% annually and sits at the 83rd percentile of its 52-week range, yet the last quarterly gain slowed to +3.6%.

Without valuation multiples (PE, EV/EBIT, P/B, FCF yield are all unavailable), the risk/reward cannot be formally quantified. What the numbers show is a stock that has re-rated significantly while its nearest-quarter earnings power has deteriorated. The burden of proof falls on whether the Q2 2025 margin compression is structural or transient — a determination that **cannot be made from the supplied quantitative evidence alone.**

**Provisional stance:** The fundamental trajectory is deteriorating (margin compression, declining net income, eroding equity) while price momentum has decelerated. The risk/reward skews unfavorably absent evidence of margin stabilization or re-expansion. The key swing factor — the cause of the Q2 2025 margin collapse — is unidentifiable from the numbers provided.
V.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Equity Research Report: Visa Inc. (V)**
**Decision Date: 2025-07-01**

### 1. Business / Franchise Read
Based on its latest 10-K filing (2024-11-13), Visa operates as a global leader in digital payments, facilitating transactions across more than 200 countries via its VisaNet network. It operates primarily as a network provider in a "four-party" model, connecting consumers, merchants, financial institutions, and governments, without bearing credit risk from card issuance. The franchise is characterized by massive scale (processing 234 billion transactions in FY2024), a vast global acceptance network (~150 million merchant locations), and significant network effects driven by 4.6 billion payment credentials. Its strategy focuses on three pillars: 1) growing core consumer payments by converting cash and checks, 2) expanding into new flows (P2P, B2C, B2B, G2C) via platforms like Visa Direct, and 3) diversifying revenue through over 200 value-added services (e.g., issuing processing, risk solutions, advisory). The business model is asset-light, with revenue derived from service, data processing, and international transaction fees.

### 2. Recent Developments (Point-in-Time Evidence)
*   **Narrative & Investor Sentiment (June 2025):** Visa is repeatedly highlighted in financial articles (2025-06-18, 2025-06-16, 2025-06-15) as a durable, high-quality holding due to its powerful network effect and leadership in digital payments. An article (2025-06-14) projects Visa as a potential candidate to surpass Nvidia's market cap by 2035.
*   **Operational Performance:** A web context article (2025-06-05) notes a "clear resurgence in international travel" driving cross-border transaction volumes, a key growth lever. Another (2025-06-14) cites Visa's Q1 2025 earnings (reported earlier) showing net revenue up 10% YoY and cross-border volumes surging 16%.
*   **Strategic Execution:** Web contexts (2025-06-06, 2025-06-14) indicate Visa is accelerating product innovation, specifically noting "AI-ready cards" and investments in stablecoins. The 10-K (2024-11-13) details ongoing strategic investments in Tap to Pay, tokenization, Visa Direct, and Open Banking via Tink.
*   **Corporate Action & Legal:** Visa defeated a class-action lawsuit regarding "Vanilla" gift card scams on 2025-06-23. However, on 2025-06-24, a judge rejected Visa's motion to dismiss a separate U.S. Department of Justice debit card antitrust lawsuit, allowing it to proceed to a "rule of reason" trial.

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
**Risks:**
*   **Regulatory & Legal Pressure:** A significant ongoing risk is the active antitrust lawsuit from the U.S. DOJ (2025-06-24) alleging anti-competitive debit routing practices. A web context (2025-05-29) also references a "transatlantic antitrust storm" with the EU. Adverse outcomes could fundamentally restructure business practices or levy substantial fines.
*   **Valuation Concerns:** Multiple recent articles (2025-06-16, 2025-06-15) explicitly flag Visa's high valuation as a potential constraint on future investor returns, despite its strong fundamentals.
*   **Competitive & Disruptive Threats:** The 10-K and news snippets acknowledge the evolving payments landscape with fintechs, digital wallets, and real-time payment schemes. Web contexts mention competition from blockchain-based platforms (2025-06-14) and cryptocurrencies (2025-06-28).

**Offsets:**
*   **Strong Network Effects & Moat:** The franchise benefits from deep entrenchment, high switching costs, and a ubiquitous brand, repeatedly cited in the 10-K and investor narratives as a key defense.
*   **Diversification and Growth Levers:** The strategy to grow beyond core C2B payments into new flows (Visa Direct, B2B) and value-added services provides multiple growth avenues, reducing reliance on any single segment.
*   **Proven Legal and Strategic Navigation:** The company has a history of navigating complex regulations and has recently won related legal battles (2025-06-23), demonstrating resilience.

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Positive, with noted legal overhang.**
The supplied evidence confirms Visa maintains a dominant, scalable, and strategically expanding franchise at the center of secular digital payment growth. Recent narrative and operational data point to strong fundamental momentum, particularly in cross-border transactions. However, the qualitative outlook is tempered by the material risk posed by the ongoing U.S. DOJ antitrust lawsuit and broader regulatory scrutiny, the outcome of which is uncertain and could impair the business model. The high valuation noted in the narrative also suggests this positive view may be broadly priced in. **Confirmed: Dominant network, growth in new flows, active antitrust litigation. Inference: The outcome of the DOJ lawsuit is the primary near-term qualitative wildcard for the franchise's risk profile.**

corrections.issue: The qualitative report states 'Q1 2025 earnings report revealed robust momentum, with net revenue rising 10% year-over-year to $9.51 billion' (from a web context article dated 2025-06-14). However, the raw quantitative evidence shows Q1 2025 (2024-12-31) revenue was $9.51B, and the YoY comparison would be against 2023-12-31 revenue of $8.63B, which is +10.2% — this is consistent. But the qualitative report also cites 'Visa reported $9.6 billion in revenue, up 9% year-over-year, and EPS of $2.76' from a 2025-05-13 article. The $9.6B figure does not match any single quarter in the raw data (Q4 2024 at $9.62B is closest). The EPS of $2.76 is not verifiable from the raw packs (EPS is marked n/a throughout). The qualitative specialist should note that EPS figures cited in news articles cannot be verified from supplied evidence.
corrections.required_action: Acknowledge that the $2.76 EPS figure from the investdiva.com article cannot be verified from the supplied quantitative pack (EPS is n/a for all periods). This is a minor issue as it comes from a third-party news source, not the specialist's own analysis.

corrections.issue: The quantitative report states 'Net income has declined in two of the last three quarters on a YoY basis (Q2 2025: −1.8%; Q3 2024 is not directly comparable to the prior-year series shown, but Q4 2024 at $5.32B is below Q4 2023's implied run-rate).' The claim about Q4 2024 is misleading. The raw data shows Q4 2024 (2024-09-30) net income = $5.32B, and the comparable prior-year quarter Q4 2023 (2023-09-30) net income = $4.68B. This is actually a +13.7% YoY increase, not a decline. The quant report's framing suggests Q4 2024 earnings were weak relative to Q4 2023, which is incorrect based on the supplied data.
corrections.required_action: Correct the Q4 2024 vs Q4 2023 comparison: net income grew from $4.68B to $5.32B (+13.7% YoY), contradicting the implication of decline. The 'two of the last three quarters' claim for YoY net income declines is not supported by the data.

corrections.issue: The qualitative report's section on 'Valuation Concerns' cites 'Multiple recent articles (2025-06-16, 2025-06-15) explicitly flag Visa's high valuation as a potential constraint on future investor returns.' This is accurately sourced from the news pack. However, the quantitative report notes that no valuation multiples (PE, EV/EBIT, P/B, FCF yield) are available in the raw data. The qualitative report does not flag this gap — it presents valuation concerns as narrative-driven without noting that the quantitative team cannot verify or contextualize these claims with actual multiples. This is a minor omission but worth noting for the PM.
corrections.required_action: No action required — this is a cross-report observation for PM awareness. The qualitative report accurately cites news sources, and the quantitative report correctly flags the absence of valuation multiples.

unsupported_claims: The quantitative report claims 'Q4 2024 at $5.32B is below Q4 2023's implied run-rate' — this is unsupported. Raw data shows Q4 2023 (2023-09-30) net income = $4.68B, which is below Q4 2024's $5.32B. The claim of a decline is factually incorrect per the supplied evidence.
The qualitative report cites 'EPS of $2.76' from a news article (2025-05-13, investdiva.com) — this figure cannot be verified from the raw quantitative pack where EPS is marked n/a for all periods.

The unresolved DOJ antitrust lawsuit adds further uncertainty to an already fully-valued name.

U.S. Federal Laws and Regulation
When we contract with the federal government, we are subject to federal laws and regulations relating to the award, administration and performance of U.S. government contracts. CMS regulates our UnitedHealthcare businesses and certain aspects of our Optum businesses. Payments by CMS to our businesses are subject to regulations, including those governing fee-for-service and the submission of information relating to the health status of enrollees for purposes of determining the amounts of certain payments to us. CMS also has the right to audit our performance to determine our compliance with CMS contracts and regulations and the quality of care we provide to Medicare beneficiaries. Our commercial business is further subject to CMS audits related to medical loss ratios (MLRs) and risk adjustment data.
UnitedHealthcare Community & State has Medicaid and CHIP contracts, which are subject to federal regulations regarding services to be provided to Medicaid enrollees, payment for those services and other aspects of these programs. There are many regulations affecting Medicare and Medicaid compliance, and the regulatory environment with respect to these programs is complex.
Our businesses are also subject to laws and regulations relating to consumer protection, anti-fraud and abuse, anti-kickbacks, false claims, prohibited referrals, inappropriate reduction or limitation of health care services, anti-money laundering and securities and antitrust compliance.
Privacy, Security and Data Standards Regulation.
Certain of our operations are subject to regulation under the administrative simplification provisions of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, as amended (HIPAA), which apply to both the group and individual health insurance markets, including self-funded employee benefit plans. Federal regulations related to HIPAA contain minimum standards for electronic transactions and code sets and for the privacy and security of protected health information.
Our businesses must comply with the Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act (HITECH), which regulates matters relating to privacy, security and data standards. HITECH imposes requirements on uses and disclosures of health information; includes contracting requirements for HIPAA business associate agreements; extends parts of HIPAA privacy and security provisions to business associates; adds federal data breach notification requirements for covered entities and business associates and reporting requirements to HHS and the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and, in some cases, to the local media; strengthens enforcement and imposes higher financial penalties for HIPAA violations and, in certain cases, imposes criminal penalties for individuals, including employees. In the conduct of our business, depending on the circumstances, we may act as either a covered entity or a business associate.
5
Table of Contents
The use and disclosure of individually identifiable health data by our businesses are also regulated in some instances by other federal laws, including the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act (GLBA) or state statutes implementing GLBA. These federal laws and state statutes generally require insurers to provide customers with notice regarding how their non-public personal health and financial information is used and the opportunity to "opt out" of certain disclosures before the insurer shares such information with a third party, and generally prescribe safeguards for the protection of personal information. Neither the GLBA nor HIPAA privacy regulations preempt more stringent state laws and regulations, which may apply to us, as discussed below. Federal consumer protection laws may also apply in some instances to privacy and security practices related to personally identifiable information.
ERISA.
The Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974, as amended (ERISA), regulates how our services are provided to or through certain types of employer-sponsored health benefit plans. ERISA is a set of laws and regulations subject to interpretation by the U.S. Department of Labor (DOL) as well as the federal courts. ERISA sets forth standards on how our business units may do business with employers who sponsor employee health benefit plans, particularly those who maintain self-funded plans. Regulations established by the DOL subject us to additional requirements for administration of benefits, claims payment and member appeals under health care plans governed by ERISA.
State Laws and Regulation
Health Care Regulation.
Our insurance and HMO subsidiaries must be licensed by the jurisdictions in which they conduct business. All of the states in which our subsidiaries offer insurance and HMO products regulate those products and operations. The states require periodic financial reports and establish minimum capital or restricted cash reserve requirements. The National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) has adopted model regulations, which require expanded governance practices and risk and solvency assessment reporting. Most states have adopted these or similar measures to expand the scope of regulations relating to corporate governance and internal control activities of HMOs and insurance companies. We are required to maintain a risk management framework and file a confidential self-assessment report with state insurance regulators. We file reports annually with Connecticut, our lead regulator, and with New York, as required by the state's regulation.
Our health plans and insurance companies are regulated under state insurance holding company regulations. Such regulations generally require registration with applicable state departments of insurance and the filing of reports describing capital structure, ownership, financial condition, certain affiliated transactions and general business operations. Most state insurance holding company laws and regulations require prior regulatory approval of acquisitions and material affiliated transfers of assets, as well as transactions between the regulated companies and their parent holding companies or affiliates. These laws may restrict the ability of our regulated subsidiaries to pay dividends to our holding companies.
Some of our business activity is subject to other health care-related regulations and requirements, including PPO, Managed Care Organization (MCO), utilization review (UR), TPA, pharmacy care services, durable medical equipment or care provider-related regulations and licensure requirements. These regulations differ from state to state and may contain network, contracting, product and rate, licensing and financial and reporting requirements. Health care-related laws and regulations set specific standards for delivery of services, appeals, grievances and payment of claims, adequacy of health care professional networks, fraud prevention, protection of consumer health information, pricing and underwriting practices and covered benefits and services. State health care anti-fraud and abuse prohibitions encompass a wide range of activities, including kickbacks for referral of members, billing for unnecessary medical services and improper marketing. Certain of our businesses are subject to state general agent, broker and sales distribution laws and regulations. UnitedHealthcare Community & State and certain o
